primary_sources.title: Tribunal Report on the Removal of Tun Salleh Abas, August 1988
Salleh Abas (former Lord President) v Government of Malaysia, High Court suit

amendment_text_change.before: the judicial power of the Federation shall be vested in two High Courts ... and in such inferior courts as may be provided by federal law
amendment_text_change.after: There shall be two High Courts ... and the High Courts and inferior courts shall have such jurisdiction and powers as may be conferred by or under federal law

notes: The 1988 judicial crisis. PM Mahathir engineers the removal of Lord President Salleh Abas via constitutional tribunal procedure; five Supreme Court judges suspended, two dismissed. Article 121(1) is amended to remove the phrase 'judicial power of the Federation', textually neutering the doctrine of separation of powers as understood pre-1988. Federal Court doctrine of basic-structure-recovery only resumes post-2017 (Semenyih Jaya). Legitimacy delta to the Federal Court is the largest single negative shock in its post-independence history. Encoded as a single ConstitutionalAmendment event though the institutional consequences span months.
